title: Start
body:
Welcome to my greenhouse!
Seems like you're here for something in particular.
...
Oh yes! I was busy making some cherry syrup yesterday.
The cherry tree I was growing awhile ago finally started bearing fruits!
And from what I heard, you're in need of an ingredient for the children who visited recently, isn't it?
That being said...
Perhaps I could give you the syrup...
But first, I'd like to ask you a few questions.
Just to make sure my dear syrup is in good hands.
So! I'd like you to answer some questions about botany, pretty please with a cherry on top?
[[ Yeah, sure.|Question1]]

title: Question1
body:
Great! I knew you'd agree to it hehe.
Let's start.
Question 1: Which of these terms is used to describe the means by which the male and female cells combine to form a new cell which then eventually develops into a new embryo?
[[ Respiration |wrong1]]
[[ Ptyxis |wrong2]]
[[ Photosynthesis |wrong3]]
[[ Fertilization |correct]]

title: wrong1
body:
Wroooong.
In botany, ptyxis refers to the specific way an individual leaf is folded, rolled, or coiled while inside a vegetative bud!
It occurs when the plant is newly emerging, typically during early spring.
Moving on...
 [[|Question2]]

title: Question2
body: yo you dumb as shit lol
